codeReview.summary: The new multi-device spec verifies rotated key material and a successful sign round-trip on rotated shares, but it does not cover the full rotate→distribute→adopt path and does not directly verify rejection of old shares after rotation.

issues.description: The test explicitly models adoption by direct runtime seeding (`__iglooTestSeedRuntime`) instead of driving rotated-share distribution/adoption. This misses regressions in the required rotate→distribute→adopt integration path and therefore does not fully satisfy the expected behavior.

Expected behavior requires proving old shares are no longer valid after rotation. The spec only checks rotated share/member material differs from originals and never performs a protocol attempt with old shares against the rotated keyset to assert failure, so old-share invalidation is inferred rather than verified.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The assigned `protocol-flow-worker` skill is scoped to source-side onboarding sponsorship and mandates agent-browser ceremony verification, but this feature implemented a rotate-keyset regression gate via Playwright-only workflow. Skill scope/procedure appears mismatched for this feature type.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/protocol-flow-worker/SKILL.md:3,21,42,58 compared with transcript skeleton for workerSessionId 32c527f3-401e-471d-b0fa-b049bba56d4a (protocol-flow-worker invoked; validation done with tsc/vitest/playwright commands, no agent-browser step).

summary: Fail. The implementation adds valuable rotated-keyset signing coverage, but it leaves two blocking gaps relative to the feature expectations: no direct distribution/adoption-path validation and no explicit old-share invalidation assertion.
